Happy endings

Happy endings, hard-won and joyful.

These stories hold both sides of rescue: the difficult beginnings and the everyday happiness that comes after a dog is safe.

Start with Mercy Help another dog

Rescue stories

Every adopted dog gets to become more than their past.

We are starting with Mercy, Rosa, and Betty. More stories will join them as Shoshi shares labeled photos, videos, and adopter updates.

Adopted

Mercy

Mercy's story began in heartbreaking conditions, but it did not end there. With medical care, patience, and a safe home, she became more than what happened to her.

Read the story

Adopted

Rosa

Rosa needed steady medical care, patience, and someone willing to believe her story was not finished. Today she is a happy ending with a life beyond the injury.

Read the story

Adopted

Betty

Betty the mastiff needed patience, structure, and a home prepared for her size and needs. Her adoption shows how transparency helps dogs find the right people.

Read the story
Ways to help

It takes community.

01 Adopt Start with the dog in front of you, then we help decide whether the fit is right. 02 Foster Give a dog a calm place to land while they get ready for adoption. 03 Volunteer Help with walks, transport, care, events, photos, admin, or remote support. 04 Donate One-time and monthly gifts both matter. No amount is too small to help.
Underdog Heroes

Dog rescue for abused, neglected, and overlooked dogs who deserve a fighting chance.

Adopt: Adopt@underdogheroes.org Foster: Foster@underdogheroes.org Volunteer: info@underdogheroes.org Instagram
